results.title: Brown & Brown AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Brown & Brown, Inc., one of the largest insurance brokers in the U.S., has positioned itself as a leader in AI transformation within the insurance industry[1]. The company has developed comprehensive AI initiatives spanning multiple business functions, from employee benefits and claims management to proprietary AI tool development[2]. With partnerships including EXL, Anthropic, and OnCorps, Brown & Brown is leveraging AI to streamline claims processing, automate underwriting, and enhance customer experiences[3]. The company has appointed dedicated AI leadership, including a Chief Data Officer and technology innovation executives, demonstrating significant organizational commitment to AI adoption[4]. Brown & Brown's approach focuses on responsible AI deployment with emphasis on transparency, governance, and risk management, as outlined in their comprehensive AI regulation white papers[5].

What AI initiatives has Brown & Brown implemented?

The company has developed comprehensive AI

Key partnerships include EXL for disability claims management AI, OnCorps through BBH Connectors for business process automation, and Certificate Hero for insurance certificate automation. The company also references partnerships with technology leaders like Anthropic.

Brown & Brown appointed Michael Vaughan as Chief Data Officer in 2023 and Stephen Boyd as Senior VP of Technology, Innovation, and Digital Strategy to lead AI and technology transformation initiatives across the organization.

results.title: Team Lead, Enterprise Data Protection | Jobflarely
highlights: Data Protection to join our growing team in Daytona Beach, FL, Dallas, TX and Atlanta, GA! Reporting to the Chief Data Officer, this high-impact role will shape and drive the strategy, execution, and governance required to protect sensitive client and company data in a hybrid-cloud, global operating model. How You Will Contribute: Develop & Lead Strategy: Implement the Enterprise Data Protection Strategy, working in close partnership with the Chief Data Officer (CDO), Chief Information Security Officer (CISO), and Chief Technology Officer (CTO). Build a Scalable Practice: Launch and scale a Data Privacy & Protection practice that covers discovery, classification, tagging, loss prevention, and encryption — aligned to evolving regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA). Operationalize Controls: Define and oversee implementation of enterprise-wide policies, standards, technologies, and metrics to secure sensitive data wherever it resides — from on-prem to cloud to endpoint. Establish Inventory & Classification Frameworks: Develop and maintain a comprehensive inventory of data assets, implementing risk-aligned classification and protection controls. Enable the Business: Partner with delivery teams and business leaders to embed security into enablement, ensuring that privacy and protection accelerate — not inhibit — the pace of innovation. Drive Awareness & Accountability: Serve as the evangelist for data protection across the enterprise, creating and delivering training, awareness, and change management programs. Skills & Experience to be Successful: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Security, or a related field (or equivalent experience) 8+ years of progressive experience in data protection, privacy, or security leadership 15+ years of relevant experience with 5+ years in progressive leadership roles (Preferred) Direct experience securing data across multi-national environments with geo-specific regulations (e.g., GDPR, LGPD, etc.) (Preferred) Experience with Microsoft M365, hybrid cloud data centers, and related architecture (Preferred) Industry experience in insurance, financial services, or other highly regulated environments (Preferred) Expertise in Data Loss Prevention (DLP) strategy and implementation Deep understanding of data discovery, classification, tagging, protection, and related risks and controls Hands-on experience with leading platforms such as Proofpoint, BigID, Varonis, etc. Proven experience writing and socializing policies and governance frameworks at scale Strong communication, facilitation, and cross-functional leadership skills Experience managing and integrating third-party managed services for delivery at scale Proven track record in matrixed, global, and hybrid team environments #IH Teammate Benefits & Total Well-Being We go beyond standard benefits, focusing on the total well-being of our teammates, including: Health Benefits: Medical/Rx, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, Disability Insurance Financial Benefits: ESPP; 401k; Student Loan Assistance; Tuition Reimbursement Mental Health & Wellness: Free Mental Health & Enhanced Advocacy Services Beyond Benefits: Paid Time Off, Holidays, Preferred Partner Discounts and more. Not reflective of all benefits. Enrollment waiting periods or eligibility criteria may apply to certain benefits. Benefit details and offerings may vary for subsidiary entities or in specific geographic locations. The Power To Be Yourself As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment comprised of people from all backgrounds, with a variety of experiences and perspectives, guided by our Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging (DIB) motto, “The Power to Be Yourself”. We think of ourselves as a team, so we have teammates - not employees. We strive to attract people who are competitive, driven, and disciplined. Built on meritocracy, our unique company culture rewards self-starters and those who are committed to doing what is best

results.title: Analyst, Employee Benefits Consulting – Career Connections  | Villanova University
highlights:
Analyst, Employee Benefits Consulting – Career Connections | Villanova University
...
Brown & Brown, Southborough
...
# Analyst, Employee Benefits Consulting
...
Job Overview: Analyst – Employee Benefits Consulting
...
Brown & Brown, Southborough
...
Brown & Brown, Southborough (B&B) is a leading health and welfare employee benefits consulting firm. Founded in 2002 as Strategic Benefit Advisors and acquired by Brown & Brown (NYSE: BRO) in 2015, our firm has expanded its reach and become a national leader in employee benefits consulting. B&B works with a diverse group of regional, national, and global employers – from household names in life sciences, technology, and health care, to many of the top colleges and universities.
...
Make an impact. Employee benefits are a central part of people’s lives and key to an employer’s ability to attract and retain talent. Employee benefits also have the power to impact an individual’s physical, emotional, and financial well-being, and organizations’ diversity, equity, and inclusion goals. B&B is at the center of these important pursuits. To supplement our core consulting practice and better serve our clients, we have built out several robust “specialty” teams, including data analytics, population health, clinical practice, well-being, communication and engagement, and actuarial science and benefit finance.
...
Build your career. While we have the strength and resources of a 17,000+ teammate, Fortune 1000 company, our office in Framingham operates independently and gives teammates of all levels the ability to interact with our diverse group of clients. We are recruiting a new class of college graduates to support our growing benefits consulting practice. College hires are a key part of our future success, and we’ve curated an extensive training program to develop the next generation of leaders geared toward generalist health and welfare consulting. Building upon this foundational training, you will have the opportunity to pursue various specialty tracks (as noted above) in addition to the day-to-day work as a generalist consultant.
...
How You’ll Spend Your Time
...
The successful candidate will be an integral member of the B&B team, consulting with clients on all aspects of health and welfare benefits. Primary job responsibilities include:
...
- Manage complex projects and client deliverables
- Work with client teams to help develop multi-year strategic plans for employer clients
- Research emerging trends in the national healthcare and employee benefits landscape
- Analyze financial metrics related to health and welfare benefit plans (e.g., medical, dental, vision, well-being, disability, etc.)
- Support B&B operations through ad-hoc data analysis
- Provide technical assistance for the firm’s ongoing projects
- Support and execute on vendor management related tasks on behalf of our employer clients
...
The Qualifications You’ll Bring
...
Successful candidates will possess the following attributes and experience:
...
- A bachelor’s degree from a competitive college or university; minimum 3.3 cumulative GPA
- 0-3 years of prior work experience
- Curiosity and eagerness to learn
- Excellent communication and organizational skills
- Strong interpersonal skills such as problem-solving, knowledge-sharing, and teamwork
- Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
- An interest in healthcare related fields

# Data Scientist, GenAI
...
Atlanta, GA, USA
...
At AIG, we are making long-term investments in a brand-new, innovative Generative AI team, designed to explore new possibilities for how artificial intelligence can be applied in insurance and beyond, and we need your help.
...
With the support and investment needed to explore new frontiers in generative AI, you'll be working alongside talented colleagues, innovating and leading projects that will transform how we manage risk and serve our customers.
...
central to our
...
engineering and product
...
As a Data Scientist at AIG, you will serve a critical role in the architecture, development, and delivery of impactful enterprise-level Generative AI solutions. We are seeking a highly technical individual contributor with extensive hands-on experience, as we are an applied data science team operating in an agile and production-oriented environment. You will be focused on Large Language Models (LLMs) and Machine Learning (ML), acting as a primary technical driver for our most complex projects.
...
End-to-End Development: Lead the development and successful delivery of data science and generative AI solutions in accordance with business requirements.
...
Production Oversight: Monitor solutions in production to ensure performance, reliability, and accuracy.
...
Technical Collaboration: Partner with cross-functional teams including product managers, engineers, and business leaders to translate requirements into technical reality.
...
Evaluation & Quality: Build robust evaluation frameworks to measure LLM efficacy, manage ground truth dataset quality, and guide the product development roadmap.
...
Architecture: Design scalable ML pipelines and RAG frameworks that integrate seamlessly with enterprise data structures.
...
5+ years of experience in a data science role, with a strong emphasis on NLP and ML, working in an agile production-oriented environment.
...
Proven Delivery: Experience successfully delivering multiple GenAI, analytical, or ML projects from conception through to production.
...
Python Expertise: Strong, expert-level proficiency in Python and its data science ecosystem (e.g., PyTorch, Pandas, Scikit-learn).
...
LLM Mastery: 5+ years of practical experience with open-source Large Language Models (Llama 3, Mixtral, etc.), including prompt engineering, inference optimization, and fine-tuning.
...
GenAI Solutions: 3+ years of experience building Generative AI Solutions, including designing and building RAG frameworks, validation pipelines, observability, and monitoring solutions.
...
Education: Master's degree in data science, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field.
...
Palantir Platform Experience: Hands-on experience or certification in the Palantir platform (Foundry/AIP).
...
Ontology Mastery: A strong understanding of Ontology—specifically how to map complex real-world data entities and relationships into a digital twin framework to power AI applications.
...
A "hands-on" mindset with the desire to be a primary technical lead through code quality and architectural excellence.

Job Title: Data Scientist / Machine Learning Engineer (Generative AI Focus)Contract Length: 12+ Months Hybrid schedule 3 days per week onsite/ 2 remoteLocation: Charlotte, NC/ Irving, TX/ Boston, MA

We are seeking a highly motivated Data Scientist / Machine Learning Engineer to build advanced analytics and Generative AI (Gen AI) solutions across multiple business functions. This role combines strong data analysis capabilities with machine learning and emerging Gen AI techniques to drive business insights, automation, and innovation.
...
The ideal candidate is hands-on, analytical, and comfortable owning the full lifecycle of data science solutions—from problem definition through model development and deployment—while collaborating closely with engineering and business stakeholders.
...
- Perform in-depth data analysis and exploration using SQL and statistical techniques to uncover patterns, solve business problems, and support data-driven decision-making.
...
- Design, develop, and implement scalable solutions using Python or Java.
...
- Utilize data science and machine learning libraries such as NumPy, SciPy, Matplotlib, and Scikit-learn.
...
- Build reusable pipelines for data processing, feature engineering, and model evaluation.
...
- Develop and evaluate machine learning models, including tree-based and ensemble algorithms such as Random Forest and XGBoost.
...
- Assess model performance, tune hyperparameters, and ensure models meet business and technical requirements.
...
- Apply AI-assisted techniques to enhance productivity and insights.
...
- Craft effective prompts using Gemini or similar generative AI models to support data exploration, feature generation, analysis, and summarization.
...
- Strong SQL and data analysis skills.
- Experience working with structured and semi-structured datasets.
- Proficiency in Python or Java for data science, machine learning, and analytical workloads.
- Hands-on experience with machine learning frameworks and model development.
- Experience building, training, and evaluating predictive models in production or near-production environments.
...
- Ability to work independently and own initiatives end-to-end, from problem definition and requirements gathering through solution delivery and validation.
- Experience using generative AI models to augment analytical workflows.
- Familiarity with prompt engineering.
- Experience leveraging large language models (LLMs) for automation and analytical tasks.
- Experience integrating Gen AI capabilities into analytical processes.

Generative AI Focus

- Develop and deploy Gen AI solutions that enhance productivity, automate workflows, and generate AI-driven business insights.
- Apply foundational knowledge of Gen AI concepts, tools, and use cases.
- Experience with large language models (LLMs), prompt engineering, or AI-assisted analytics.
- Strong interest in emerging AI technologies and a willingness to continuously learn and apply new Gen AI innovations.
...
Ideal Candidate Profile
...
- Self-driven data professional with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
- Combines practical machine learning expertise with emerging AI capabilities.
- Comfortable navigating ambiguous problems and translating business needs into technical solutions.
- Capable of delivering measurable business outcomes.
- Strong communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
- Passionate about applying traditional machine learning and modern Generative AI techniques to solve complex business challenges.

highlights:
AI Architect | GenAI · Agentic AI · LLMOps · Monitoring · Observability · Governance | Building production LLM systems across Azure · AWS · GCP | 10+ yrs across BFSI, Healthcare, Energy | AWS SA – Professional
...
AI Architect at Brown Brothers Harriman
...
AI Architect with 10+ years designing and shipping production AI/ML and Generative AI platforms for regulated enterprises across Banking, Insurance, Healthcare, and Energy. Currently leading the enterprise GenAI platform at Brown Brothers Harriman — own the firm's agentic AI standard, gate every use-case through architecture review and model-risk governance, and act as the single technical point of contact between executive sponsors and engineering. Previously AI Solutions Architect at Marathon Petroleum, Senior ML Engineer at USAA, and OMOP CDM lead at Millipore Sigma (the Merck Group). I operate as a lead-from-the-front individual contributor — own architecture end-to-end, build the hardest pieces hands-on, mentor mid-level engineers, and present roadmap, risk, and ROI to leadership. Open to senior IC roles in Applied AI, Forward-Deployed Engineering, AI Architecture, and Member-of-Technical-Staff tracks. SKILLS: • LLMS & GENAI: Claude Sonnet 4.7 (1M-context), GPT-4o + o-series, Gemini 2.5, LLaMA 4; prompt engineering, RAG (hybrid BM25+vector, Cohere Rerank), hallucination mitigation (citations, refusal routing) • AGENTIC AI: LangGraph, CrewAI, Microsoft Agent Framework, Google ADK, MCP (Model Context Protocol), A2A; multi-agent orchestration (planner / retriever / action / reviewer) • LLMOPS & EVAL: RAGAS, LangSmith, Promptfoo, LLM-as-judge; eval-gated promotion; LiteLLM gateway with model routing + caching; LangFuse + OpenTelemetry observability • GUARDRAILS & GOVERNANCE: Nemo Guardrails, Microsoft Presidio (PII/PHI); SR 11-7, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, EU AI Act, HIPAA-aware data flows • INFERENCE & TRAINING: vLLM (continuous batching, paged attention, KV-cache); FSDP, LoRA, QLoRA, DPO; HF PEFT + TRL; A100 / H100 • CLOUD (MULTI-CLOUD PRODUCTION): Azure (AI Foundry, Azure OpenAI, AI Search, AKS, ML Studio), AWS (Bedrock + AgentCore, SageMaker, Textract), GCP (Vertex AI + Agent Builder, BigQuery) • BACKEND & STACK: Python (async, Pydantic v2, FastAPI), REST APIs, PostgreSQL, Snowflake, Delta Lake, Databricks, PySpark, Kafka, Airflow • MLOPS & DEVOPS: MLflow, Azure ML Prompt Flow, Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions; Docker, Kubernetes (AKS), Terraform; blue/green + canary deployments • HEALTHCARE DATA: OMOP CDM v5.4, EHR / claims / lab integration; Power BI, Salesforce BI integrations • CERTIFICATIONS: AWS Solutions Architect — Professional · Microsoft DP-600 · Microsoft DP-203

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.
